title: Pterostylis galgula
intro: Pterostylis galgula, commonly known as the Red-beaked Greenhood, is a terrestrial orchid species native to Australia. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including inflammation and respiratory problems, due to its potential anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Pterostylis galgula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and iridoids, which are believed to contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Pterostylis galgula is often prepared as a tincture or infused in hot water as a tea to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Pterostylis galgula is a species of orchid in the family Orchidaceae. It is native to Australia and is a terrestrial orchid.
